LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BLEY & BLEY, SAMANTHA DEJEAN, AND STEPHEN BLEY'S MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SEC. 128.7, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS STRICKEN AS IT IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (SEE CODE CIV. PRO. 340.6, SUBD.(A).) PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SET FORTH A COGNIZABLE BASIS FOR TOLLING. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,653 ARE AWARDED. DEFENDANTS TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2015 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BLEY & BLEY, SAMANTHA DEJEAN, AND STEPHEN BLEY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted. No opposition filed. Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. ? 128.7
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KEVIN SULLIVAN, ESQ. of Griffin & Sullivan, Tel No. 415-397-2600 appearing with Plaintiff Gilbert Papazian (Sarah Domenico [CSR# 13652] present providing Mr. Papazian with real-time transcription assistance);
Atty. ADRIAN T. LAMBIE, ESQ. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel No. 415-788-1900 appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS BLEY & BLEY, SAMANTHA DEJEAN, AND STEPHEN BLEY'S Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. Sec. 128.7, Defendants' Motion to Strike is GRANTED. Defendants' Motion for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is stricken as it is barred by the statute of limitations. (See Code Civ. Pro. 340.6, subd.(a).) Plaintiff fails to set forth a cognizable basis for tolling. Sanctions in the amount of $4,653 are awarded. Defendants to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. ? 128.7
Off Calendar Aug-12-2015 Continued To Sep-08-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows:
DEFENDANT BLEY & BLEY, SAMANTHA DEJEAN, STEPHEN BLEY'S Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. 128.7 is continued to September 8, 2015 per the agreement of the parties. Opposition papers were delivered in court today. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
